customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high curiosity. Eva is an engaged new homeowner motivated by bill pain. She asked detailed due-diligence questions (roof damage, seasonal performance, repair costs) but showed no skepticism about Ion or solar generally. No scam anxiety, no spouse coordination needed, no financial vulnerability disclosed.
unresolved_concerns: Cost of repair / warranty coverage — Eva asked explicitly at 04:44; Joel correctly deferred to the specialist but noted it as a question to address.
Exact savings amount — Eva asked 'I'll be saving every month because of it?' at 11:05; Joel confirmed the specialist will show the numbers but gave no anchor.
sensitive_topics_raised: Bill amounts in transcript appear as '$3.50' (average) and '$3.90' (last bill) — these are almost certainly $350 and $390 respectively, consistent with Joel's confirmation 'those are up there for sure.' The closer should not be confused by the truncated figures; the actual bill is approximately $390.

Transcript segment 07:57–09:10 (~75 seconds) is severely degraded and unreliable. The bill photo was successfully received (confirmed at 09:20), but any commitments or exchanges in that window cannot be verified from the transcript.
exact_promises_rep_made: Design will be built from the bill photo and sent to the design team.
Specialist will review the design and answer repair/warranty questions at the appointment.
Text with appointment day and time will be sent after the call.
Appointment confirmed: Tuesday the 28th at 7PM — BUT Joel closed verbally with 'next Wednesday' (11:18). Verify the follow-up text states Tuesday, not Wednesday, before the sit date.
closer_should_know: Joel closed the call saying 'we'll see you next Wednesday' when the appointment is Tuesday the 28th at 7PM. Eva did not correct him. Confirm the follow-up text stated Tuesday before the appointment date — if it said Wednesday, reach Eva to confirm the correct day. Eva's credit qualification was self-reported via a single 'yes' to a compound question covering both taxable income and credit score (threshold stated as 650, not 670); her credit score was never individually confirmed and should be verified at the proposal stage.
